title: monolepis chenopodioides
intro: Monolepis chenopodioides, also known as rough bladderwort or rough kidneywort, is a low-growing annual plant native to North America. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and soothe skin irritations. Monolepis chenopodioides is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or salve for its various health benefits, including its ability to aid in kidney stone relief and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract.
